title: chrysosplenium ramosum f. microphyllum
intro: Chrysosplenium ramosum f. microphyllum, also known as the small-leaved golden saxifrage or microphyllous chrysosplenium, is a rare and lesser-known variant of the Chrysosplenium ramosum plant. This perennial herb is native to Japan and is characterized by its small, rounded leaves and yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. Chrysosplenium ramosum f. microphyllum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Japanese medicine, known as Kampo, to promote overall health and well-being.
